Once you're at the IFC exit where you can head straight to Queen's Road Central, enter IFC, pass the escalator in front of you and keep walking until you come to another escalator. Go down it, turn left and it's there. If you wanna travel to Shinjuku from Tokyo station, go through the gate, go down the escalator and look at the signs. You'll see which side us bound for Shinjuku. If you wanna have a coffee at J.Cafe, take the lift to level three, go straight down the path and go past several shops until you come to an intersection. Turn right and it's there. If you want to take an elevator to a higher floor, turn left at the intersection and go past several shops until you see Pure Fitness on your left. Enter Pure Fitness and the elevator is there. I enjoy visiting the annual winter carnival where I eat sushi and go on the thrill rides, soaking up the nice Christmassy atmosphere. The sunlight has been dazzlingly hot so you might just as well have a good long soak in an outdoor onsen, soaking up the sun. it's a good bet that you go to Japan on vacation during the Easter holiday since the Japanese yen has fallen fairly rapidly over the past five months. If so, I heartily recommend you take the airport express at IFC. It's very easy. Once you're at the IFC exit where you can head straight for Queen's Road Central, enter IFC, go past the escalator in front of you and keep walking until you come to another escalator. Go down it, turn left and it's there. If you wanna go to Shinjuku from Tokyo station, go through the gate, go down the escalator and look at the signs. You'll see which side is bound for Shinjuku. If you wanna have a coffee at J.Cafe, take the elevator to level three, go straight down the path and pass several shops until you come to an intersection. Turn right and it's there. If you wanna take an elevator to a higher floor, turn left at the intersection and go past several shops until you see Pure Fitness on your left. Enter Pure Fitness and the lift is there. I enjoy visiting the annual winter carnival where I eat sushi and ride the roller coasters, soaking up the nice Christmassy atmosphere. The weather has been dazzlingly hot so you may just as well have a good long soak in an outdoor onset, soaking up the sun. In fact, I've just got out of an onsen and am toweling off. However, if you ask me whether Australia is a good place to vacation in, I wouldn't bet on it. There's nothing worth doing there. I like Japanese culture and food. I even took the JLPT at N two level. My reading far outshines my writing so the marks I lost in writing were offset by those I gained in reading. My friend took the test too. He's a little weak on speaking but strong on listening so the marks he gained in the listening section offset the marks he lost in the speaking part. After the test, we spent money on shoes and clothes like it was going out of fashion. We went to a casino and gambled like it was going out of fashion. If you enjoy being in Japan, you might spend money on cosmetic products and food like it's going out of fashion. If you enter a casino, you might gamble like it's going out of fashion like we did.

1.窮人喜歡埋怨

窮人最大的問題其實並不是窮，而是觀念落後，因為窮所以自卑，因為自卑又極度自尊，自卑與自尊絞在一起，整個人就很難相處。所以窮人心胸十分不豁達，遇事總喜歡埋怨，給身邊的人帶來負能量。

2.窮人喜歡找藉口

自古有言：“倉稟實而知禮節衣食足而知榮辱。”馬斯洛則將人的需求分為五種：生理需求，安全需求、社交需求、尊重需求和自我實現需求，當一個窮人，溫飽尚且不能解決，他會顧得上去提升自己嗎？所以窮人總喜歡給自己的失敗找藉口，而不從自身找原因。

3.窮人喜歡不付出就獲得

比貧窮更可怕的，是很多窮人安於貧窮，這就是心窮，心窮的人，不會去想自己為什麼窮，也不去想怎樣才能擺脫貧窮，他們窮得理直氣壯，他們覺得接受別人的幫助理所當然，喜歡不付出就獲得。

4.窮人疑心重

窮人信任別人的能力弱，總是懷疑別人是否會傷害自己。因為見的世面小，不能相信別人有多好，總是從別人會傷害自己的角度來思維，導致有大格局的人根本就不想和這些人相處。

It's a safe bet that you go to Japan on vacation at Easter since the Japanese yen has fallen rapidly in the last three months. If so, I thoroughly recommend taking the airport express.at IFC. It's very easy. Once you're at the IFC exit where you can head straight to Queen's Road Central, enter IFC, pass the escalator in front of you and keep walking until you come to another escalator. Go down it, turn left and go straight ahead. It's there. If you wanna go to Shinjuku from Tokyo station, go through the gate, go down the escalator and look at the signs. You'll see which side is bound for Shinjuku. If you wanna have a coffee at J.Cafe, take the lift to level three, go straight down the path until you come to an intersection, turn right and It's there. If you wanna take an elevator to a higher floor, turn left at the intersection and pass several shops until you see Pure Fitness on your left. Enter Pure Fitness and the elevator is there. I enjoy visiting the annual winter carnival where I go on the thrill rides and eat sushi, soaking up the nice Christmassy atmosphere. The sun has been dazzling so you might just as well have a good long soak in an outdoor onsen, soaking up the sun. In fact, I've just got out of an onsen, dried myself on a towel and am dressing. However, if you ask me whether Australia is a good place to vacation in, I wouldn't bet on it. There's nothing worth doing there. I like Japanese culture and food. I even took the JLPT at N one level. My listening far outshines my speaking so the marks I gained in listening offset those I lost in speaking. My friend took the test too. He's a little weak on reading but strong on writing so the marks he lost in the reading part were offset by the marks he gained in the writing section. After the test, we spent money on clothes and shoes like it was going out of fashion. We went to a casino and gambled like it was going out of fashion. If you enjoy being in Japan, you might spend money on food and cosmetic products like it's going out of fashion. If you enter a casino, you might gamble like it's going out of fashion like we did.
